message:
feat(mcp): add __fuzz_parse_tool_call_request seam behind #[cfg(feature = \"fuzz\")]

A fuzz harness for the MCP tool-call request-parsing path needs a
stable entry point that mirrors the production handler's dispatch
table: parse a JSON byte slice, locate the tool name, route to the
tool-specific Params struct, and attempt deserialization. Exposing
the handler path itself as a fuzz seam is impractical (side effects,
runtime setup, filesystem access). The smallest seam that faithfully
exercises the externally-reachable parse surface is the dispatch +
deserialize pair.

Add `__fuzz_parse_tool_call_request(input: &[u8]) -> &'static str`,
gated behind `#[cfg(feature = \"fuzz\")]` and marked `#[doc(hidden)]`
so it doesn't leak into the public rustdoc surface. The double-
underscore prefix signals \"not a stable public API\" to any caller
that stumbles across it.

Accepted wire shapes:
- {\"name\":\"wa.state\", \"arguments\":{...}}   — bare tool-call
- {\"tool\":\"wa.state\", \"arguments\":{...}}   — alternate field
- {\"method\":\"tools/call\", \"params\":{\"name\":\"wa.state\", ...}}
      — full JSON-RPC envelope shape

Returns one of the following static classification strings so the
fuzz oracle can branch on outcome without allocating:
- \"not_json\"     — input isn't valid JSON
- \"not_object\"   — top-level payload isn't an object
- \"bad_params\"   — `params` field present but not an object
- \"missing_name\" — no `name`/`tool` field present
- \"unknown_tool\" — name not in the wa.* dispatch table
- \"parsed\"       — tool Params deserialized cleanly
- \"invalid_args\" — tool is known but Params deserialize failed

Dispatch table covers every wa.* tool currently in the Mcp
handler registry: wa.search, wa.get_text, wa.send, wa.wait_for,
wa.state, wa.events, wa.events_annotate/triage/label,
wa.cass_search/cass_status/cass_view, wa.workflow_run,
wa.rules_list/rules_test, wa.tx_plan/show/run/rollback,
wa.reservations/reserve/release, wa.accounts/accounts_refresh,
wa.mission_state/explain/pause/resume/abort.

Helper shim `fuzz_parse_params_or_default<T: Default>` treats a
null `arguments` value as T::default() so tools whose Params are
entirely optional (MissionStateParams, TxPlanParams, etc.)
exercise the default-constructor path rather than returning
\"invalid_args\" when the caller omits arguments — that matches the
production tool handler behavior.

Behind the fuzz feature flag only; no change to any non-fuzz build
(release, test, bench).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
